pattern: A subsequence's bitwise AND is non-zero exactly when some single bit is set in every chosen element, so the answer is the best plain LIS length over the elements sharing any one bit.
hints: The AND of a group of numbers has a bit set only if every number in the group has that bit set - so a non-zero-AND subsequence must share at least one common bit across all its elements.
For a fixed bit position, restrict to the elements that have that bit set (keeping their original relative order) and ask: what is the longest strictly increasing subsequence among just those?
Try every bit position (about 30 of them for values under 10^9), compute the standard patience-sorting LIS length on the filtered elements for each, and take the maximum across all bits.

edge_inputs.description: every value is zero: no non-zero AND subsequence exists
repeated equal value: cannot extend beyond length one
a shared bit exists but only across a decreasing run
the whole array is increasing but its total AND is zero
